blocks.content: Preparations always take longer than planned. The goal is not to have a beautifully furnished van right away, but to ensure the basics function.

images.alt: The solar charge controller and the inverter
A stable internet connection for online work

blocks.content: There is a bed to sleep on, the electricity works and there is an internet connection, so we can leave for a test drive. But then comes an unexpected turn of events: the van is not yet fully mechanically sound, so we cannot leave yet! When traveling in a van, you always have to be able to handle unexpected twists. Adjusting plans, and above all realizing that this is not so bad at all, and that traveling is largely about this ability to adapt. The advantage now is that I can start doing that even before a test drive has begun!

The van was checked mechanically and we are ready to hit the road! There might be a few extra things that need to be done later, but for now, we are on our way. So, really picking up the van next week! And I promise I will post more photos then too.

It was actually an advantage to wait another week, because now some extra things could be done. The solar panels have been permanently attached, the wardrobe has been installed, and the gas stove has also passed its first test. Tomorrow a few final preparations, and then off for some test drives!

images.alt: When the toilet is closed, it's just a normal seat
The second seat, aka the closet

blocks.content: The long drive to Belgium went well, and then a short trip to a beautiful lake in the area. Tomorrow we leave the solid soil behind and embark on the real journey.

The first steps to get on the way were to pack all my stuff, store it in the van and clean the apartment. This was pretty intense, but with great help it all worked out very well! (Thank you W&J!!) Then most of my stuff was offloaded to give away, and some to store, and then this picture shows what's left. Before it nearly reached the ceiling of the van! You always have more stuff than you thought, even after you scaled down already in preparation to move into the van.

images.alt: Even after getting rid of most stuff, inside it's still a mess (with also some nice things! ;)

Instead of the picture above, I might also have shown this one. A bit less messy, I cleaned up just to take this picture. But what's the best to show on a blog, the messy reality or sometimes a slightly cleaner version? (What do you think? Let me know in the comments below! Oh no...:D

blocks.content: Then the real journey starts. The electricity suddenly didn't work the first day, but that turned out to be just a low battery. The water pump keeps running, so I just disconnect it when not needed. The teethinh troubles of a 'new' van. But we're on the road, first visiting the Dutch National Education Museum in Dordrecht, and then the nearby Nature reserve De Biesbosch.
